comment_text.sn4.10:1.2: The Bamboo Grove was a major monastery near Rājagaha. According to Kd 1:22.16.1, it was offered by King Bimbisāra as the very first monastery dedicated to the Saṅgha.
comment_text.sn4.10:2.4: A hundred years was the standard long life (eg. Snp 3.8:16.1, DN 14:3.30.5, AN 7.74:10.3).
comment_text.sn4.10:4.4: This echoes the idea that a wheeled chariot was unstoppable (SN 56.11:12.2), an idea arising from the Vedic conception of the Sun as a wheel.

root_text.sn4.10:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn4.10:2.2: Gamanīyo samparāyo, kattabbaṁ kusalaṁ, caritabbaṁ brahmacariyaṁ.
root_text.sn4.10:2.4: Yo, bhikkhave, ciraṁ jīvati, so vassasataṁ appaṁ vā bhiyyo”ti.
root_text.sn4.10:3.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn4.10:6.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā “jānāti maṁ bhagavā, jānāti maṁ sugato”ti dukkhī dummano tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.sn4.10:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn4.10:2.1: “Mendicants, the lifespan of humans is short.
translation_text.sn4.10:2.2: You must go to the next life. So you should do what is skillful, you should practice the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn4.10:2.4: A long life is a hundred years or a little more.”
translation_text.sn4.10:3.1: Then Māra the Wicked went up to the Buddha and addressed him in verse:
translation_text.sn4.10:4.1: “The days and nights don’t rush by,
translation_text.sn4.10:4.3: The life of mortals keeps rolling on,
translation_text.sn4.10:4.4: like a chariot’s rim around the hub.”
translation_text.sn4.10:5.2: and then life is cut short.
translation_text.sn4.10:5.3: The life of mortals wastes away,
translation_text.sn4.10:5.4: like the water in tiny streams.”
translation_text.sn4.10:6.1: Then Māra the Wicked, thinking, “The Buddha knows me! The Holy One knows me!” miserable and sad, vanished right there.
